source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 809

event.top_event: Laceration injury from contact with jagged I-beam edge
event.incident_type: Slip/Trip/Fall with Lost Time Injury (>3 days)
actions_taken: Immediate medical assessment by platform medic
X-rays and medical treatment at Medical Center
Hazard mitigation: wrapping of protruding metal pieces
Job Safety Analysis (JSA) revised to include jagged edges and uneven surfaces
event.summary: On 23 February 2025 at 0830 hours, a SafeZone worker slipped while securing felt and cleaning inside a Pressurized Welding Enclosure (PWE) on the Ursa platform at MC 809. The worker lost his footing on an elevated working surface, fell 12-18 inches, and his right leg contacted a jagged edge of an I-beam, resulting in a 3-to-4-inch laceration. The worker was medically evacuated and received 11 staples and 4 stitches. Investigation found that jagged edges and uneven surfaces were not identified in the initial JSA hazard assessment, and supervision was inadequate during the pre-job hazard identification process.
recommendations: Issue a Safety Alert regarding the incident
Conduct more thorough hazard assessments of work areas
Ensure all personnel are made aware of identified hazards before starting work
Mitigate identified hazards before work commences
Improve supervision during hazard assessment and mitigation processes
Enhance documentation of hazard analysis and job procedures on JSAs

hazards.name: Uneven working surfaces with elevation changes
Slip and trip hazards in work area

threats.name: Worker loses footing on uneven surface
Worker contacts jagged metal edge during fall

consequences.name: Lost time injury requiring medical evacuation and hospitalization

supporting_text: the initial JSA hazard identification for the work being performed did not mention jagged edges inside the work area or uneven surfaces creating hazards for personnel
Had the jagged edges and uneven surfaces been identified on the JSA and discussed with the workers prior to commencing work, the injury could have been prevented

controls.name: Fall protection personal protective equipment (SRL)
controls.barrier_role: Prevent serious injury from falls at height
controls.lod_basis: Required fall protection for work at heights

supporting_text: The IP was working at heights, which required him to wear fall protection personal protective equipment (PPE), consisting of self-retracting lifeline (SRL) at the time of the incident

the uneven surface that the IP was working on had a 12-18 inch drop down to the next working level. When the IP slipped and lost his footing, he fell 12-18 inches from the upper level to the lower level. This slight difference in elevation was not a great enough drop to deploy the IP's fall protection

controls.name: Supervision during hazard assessment and work execution
controls.barrier_role: Ensure hazards are identified and workers are aware of risks

supporting_text: The lack of supervision and these items having not been identified during the initial hazard identification allowed existing hazards to remain unaddressed throughout the operation
Supervision – Inadequate supervision while identifying the hazards in the work area during hazard assessment and mitigation for the hazards

controls.name: Hazard mitigation - removal or protection of sharp metal edges
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate or reduce exposure to jagged metal hazards
controls.lod_basis: Physical elimination or protection of identified hazards

supporting_text: the crew was able to identify the potential exposures of sharp pieces of metal that were present and mitigate the hazard by wrapping any protruding pieces of metals that were found
Hazards identified at the time off the assessment should be mitigated to reduce or eliminate the threat to personnel before starting work

controls.name: Worker awareness and training on identified hazards
controls.barrier_role: Ensure workers understand hazards and safe work practices

supporting_text: In the future, the work area should have a more thorough hazard assessment performed, and all personnel should be made aware of these hazards before starting work
Human Error – The lack of awareness by the IP concerning the uneven surfaces and foot placement during the job risk analysis and while performing the work

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
